title: ficus webbiana
intro: Ficus webbiana, also known as the Banyan Fig or Gular Banyan, is a species of fig tree native to the Indian subcontinent. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, known for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which contribute to various medicinal uses. Its health benefits include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal preparations, Ficus webbiana is often used to make teas, infusions, or topical applications, and it can also be consumed as a powder or extract.
